CT-001.description: Task 9 missing soft dep on Task 6
CT-001.evidence: Task 9 depends_on: [4, 5, 6]. Task 6 is now declared.

CT-002.evidence: Task 3 Description opens with explicit 'Wave deviation note:' paragraph explaining the move from Wave 2 to Wave 3 due to NON_DEFAULT_LOCALES import dependency on Task 2.

CT-004.evidence: Task 11 depends_on: [1, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10]. Task 7 is now present.

CT-005.description: Task 9 conditional vs unconditional mock rule for useLocalePath
CT-005.evidence: Task 9 'Authorized exceptions' section explicitly states: 'The rule is unconditional: every spec gains a mockNuxtImport identity-passthrough mock at the top of the file... mirrors the unconditional rule applied in Task 10'. All five auth page specs are individually listed in step 7 of What to do with the exact mock line. No conditionality remains.

CT-006.description: Task 9 schema computed pattern diverged from Task 10
CT-006.evidence: Task 9 Implementation hints now contains an explicit code block with 'const schemaComputed = computed(() => toTypedSchema(z.object({...})))' passed directly to 'useForm({ validationSchema: schemaComputed })' with explicit note 'Never call unref(schemaComputed), schemaComputed.value'. Matches Task 10's formulation exactly.

CT-011.description: Task 4 missing 3-level error key enumeration for profile contexts
CT-011.evidence: Task 4 'What to do' step 1 now explicitly enumerates both 2-level auth contexts AND 3-level profile contexts with label '3-level profile contexts (Task 10 contract, errors.profile.{action}.{code|generic})' listing all 5 profile actions. The AC bullet 7 adds explicit mandatory language: 'Profile-contexts use 3-level keys errors.profile.{action}.{code|generic} for actions: saveName, changePassword, resend, terminateSessions, deleteAccount. Auth-contexts use 2-level keys.' Implementation hints section has a detailed ground-truth list of all contextKey values for both auth (2-level) and profile (3-level) with exact status codes.

CT-013.description: Task 14 Test Audit missing expanded authorized exceptions list
CT-013.evidence: Task 14 'What to do' step 4 now enumerates exactly 8 authorized exception files with detailed scope: auth.global.spec.ts, login.spec.ts, register.spec.ts, reset-password.spec.ts, forgot-password.spec.ts, verify-email.spec.ts, dashboard.spec.ts, profile.spec.ts. AC bullet 3 confirms '8 authorized exceptions' with exact filenames and the limited scope of mutations allowed for each.

CT-014.description: Task 15 Pre-deploy QA missing expanded authorized exceptions awareness
CT-014.evidence: Task 15 does not contain an explicit authorized-exceptions list. However, Task 15 reads audit reports from Tasks 12-14 as inputs (depends_on: [12, 13, 14]) and cross-checks their findings. Since Task 12 and Task 14 now correctly enumerate all 8 authorized exceptions and would NOT flag them as findings, Task 15's QA will not see any false-positive blocker from these spec modifications. The downstream resolution through audit-report accuracy is sufficient. Risk: minor — if Task 15's agent independently checks spec files against AC18 without relying on audit reports, it may still miss the correct exceptions count.

CT-015.description: Task 12 Code Audit missing expanded authorized exceptions list
CT-015.evidence: Task 12 'Authorized Exceptions' section A now lists all 8 authorized exception files explicitly: auth.global.spec.ts + 7 page specs. It defines the authorized scope (identity-passthrough mock for useLocalePath only), and instructs the auditor to flag scope violations as high findings. AC bullet 7 confirms these 8 files 'not flagged IF diff limited to authorized scope'.

findings.issue: Task 15 Pre-deploy QA does not explicitly enumerate the 8 authorized exceptions to AC18/Decision 16 that Tasks 12 and 14 now correctly document. If Task 15's agent independently checks spec files against AC18 (beyond just reading audit reports), it may incorrectly flag the 7 page spec modifications as violations. Task 15 relies on Tasks 12-14 audit reports as source of truth, which is the correct indirect resolution, but the task text itself has no mention of the expanded exceptions.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is: Task 15 inherits correct exceptions context from audit reports it reads. If desired, add a note in Task 15's 'What to do' step 9 or 10 referencing 'see Tasks 12/14 authorized exceptions for AC18 scope'.

findings.issue: Task 10 Implementation hints (lines 172-190) contain a complete code block with field names, validation message keys, .refine logic, and the full useForm call — a full algorithm rather than a pattern pointer. This prescribes the exact solution. Per validator rubric, hints should point to patterns and approaches, not prescribe the solution. This was CT-017 in iteration 1 and is unchanged.
findings.fix: Replace the full implementation code block with a reference to 'see Task 9 pattern for schema computed structure' and describe the key constraints (ComputedRef without unref, refine inside computed) without enumerating field names and messages.

findings.issue: Task 9 Implementation hints contain a full code block (lines 175-199) showing the complete toTypedSchema+computed pattern with field definitions and validation keys. Similar to CT2-002 for Task 10. This was CT-018 adjacent; the code block prescribes implementation rather than pointing to a pattern. Minor by nature since the core constraint is clearly stated alongside the block.
findings.fix: The accompanying explicit rule ('Never call unref') adds enough value that the code block serves as illustration. Acceptable as documentation of the constraint. Low priority to change.

findings.issue: CT-018 from iteration 1 (spurious useApiError() call in forgot-password setup without using the handler) remains unchanged. Task 9 step 3 says 'Still inject useApiError() at the top of setup (per Decision 10) so the import is consistent across files; do not change the silent-catch UX.' This means forgot-password.vue calls useApiError() in setup but never uses the returned handler in catch. This is wasteful (one unused factory call on every page mount) but not a bug — useApiError() inside setup() is safe lifecycle-wise per Decision 10. The factory itself calls useI18n() which has zero side-effects if unused.
findings.fix: Minor: document in Task 9 or decisions.md that forgot-password's useApiError injection is intentional for pattern consistency, not functional need. Alternatively remove the injected call since forgot-password's silent catch never surfaces errors.

unchanged_from_iter1.CT-003: Confirmed as false alarm in iter 1; Task 8 dependency chain is correct.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-007: Task 7 description is sufficient; agent must preserve both useState mutation and apiMock pattern — text is clear enough.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-008: Task 9 now applies mock unconditionally (CT-005 resolved), so the vagueness about forgot-password.spec.ts is eliminated.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-009: Task 10 notes the jsdom risk for NuxtLinkLocale rendering; acceptable as a known edge case with fallback guidance.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-010: Task 3 import path fallback is correctly documented; smoke check will surface any Nitro resolution failure.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-012: Task 6 useCookie mock pattern (CT-012) is correct: mockNuxtImport factory is invoked lazily after module-level cookieRef is initialized — no issue.
unchanged_from_iter1.CT-016: Task 11 TDD Anchor vagueness on submit-vs-blur is minor and resolved by the Implementation hints recommending click submit.

summary.verdict: All 4 previous criticals resolved. All 6 targeted majors resolved. CT-015 (Task 12) and CT-013 (Task 14) resolved by explicit enumeration of 8 authorized exceptions. CT-014 (Task 15) resolved indirectly via audit-report dependency chain. No new critical or major findings introduced by the fixes. 4 minor findings remain, all pre-existing or editorial.
